items.rationale: Seen as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a way to let the 'world computer' do far more work without sacrificing decentralization; zkVMs democratize SNARK use for ordinary developers writing Rust/Go.
items.age_note: Building since 2023 (Lasso/Jolt Aug 2023), intensified through mid-2024

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped. Let's talk about why
Jolt is an insane accomplishment... a significant contribution toward the endgame solution for blockchain scalability
as you bring that Pro overhead down the number of applicable applications for snarks grows dramatically

items.rationale: Framed as the path to mainstream/consumer crypto — user-owned-and-operated physical networks where decentralized verification of physical state adds real value; backed by a fresh investment in decentralized energy.

citations.quote: The future of consumer crypto? DePIN... Crypto will win when it's synonymous with the Internet itself
leading the $9M Series A round in @daylightenergy! Daylight is building a decentralized protocol to fund and coordinate distributed energy resources
I'm excited about DePIN, payments, and new social networks

items.rationale: Crypto as counterbalance to centralized Big-Tech AI; and as the only credible infrastructure for content provenance/curation in a world of >1000x AI-generated content and deepfakes.
items.age_note: Theme since 2023 (Gensyn), reframed toward content-provenance/curation in 2024

citations.quote: there is an opportunity to >1,000x increase the amount of content published online... We need an open, global coordination layer
Decentralized blockchains are a counterbalancing force to centralized AI
NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto

items.rationale: Cheap, instant, global settlement (sub-cent USDC transfers) finally maturing; merchants save ~30% and keep direct user relationships — viewed as crypto's original and now-imminent use case.

citations.quote: Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together
merchants could save ~30% and maintain a more direct relationship with users. The benefits of crypto start becoming very obvious

items.rationale: Rollup-centric roadmap as implicit sharding; restaking unbundles cryptoeconomic security to enable 100x faster open innovation; shared sequencers (Espresso) solve fragmentation. Two flagship Series B leads in 2024.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges
Non-native DA is clearly required to scale Ethereum to its full potential in the short term
L2s are implicit sharding... now anyone can deploy an L2

items.rationale: Web3/token design unlocks community-owned IP, remix graphs, and composable brands — viewed as 'purely additive' value creation; long-standing fascination with IP marketplace design.
items.age_note: Heavy 2023 thesis; restated Feb–June 2024 (royalty design, onchain IP layer)

citations.quote: I'm extremely excited see what having a programmable onchain IP layer can unlock
Recursive, networked creativity. Multiplayer for IP. Made possible with crypto

items.rationale: Underrated primitive that lets networks recruit contributors with incentives exogenous to the protocol (e.g. ESPN resolving betting markets because of its offchain business).

citations.quote: zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto
the ability to easily introduce a key type of contributor to any network - one with incentives exogenous to the protocol

items.rationale: Underrated complement to proofs ('proofs for integrity, enclaves for privacy'); seen as a long-run foundational technology and a clear developer-platform opportunity.
items.age_note: Justin Thaler argued TEEs disruptive in 2023; restated by Eddy mid-2024

citations.quote: Enclaves (aka TEEs) are underrated. Defense in depth should apply to crypto as well. Proofs for integrity, Enclaves for privacy
There's a great opportunity to build the best developer platform for programming enclaves (TEEs) in crypto

items.rationale: Composability and user-owned data/follow-graphs make these natural; the house wants apps users truly love that close the gap between crypto owners and active onchain users.

citations.quote: Crypto needs applications with true product market fit... I'm excited about DePIN, payments, and new social networks
Let's close the gap between crypto owners and active on-chain users

items.rationale: Viewed as the single biggest force holding the industry back — deters great founders, gives ammo to politicized regulators, distorts how the public/regulators see crypto, and crowds out productive use cases. Dominant concern of mid-2024.
items.age_note: Long-running anti-speculation stance, escalated sharply Mar–Apr 2024 during the memecoin wave

citations.quote: we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect
Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails
We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back

items.rationale: Persistent view that SEC uses uncertainty and enforcement as leverage to ban/offshore the technology ('lawfare, not the rule of law'); seen as national-security risk if innovation moves abroad. Tempered in 2024 by legislative progress.
items.age_note: Intense hostility throughout 2023; shifted toward cautious optimism in 2024 as FIT21 passed the House and the ETH ETF was approved

citations.quote: These antics are further evidence that these enforcement actions are about lawfare, not the rule of law
One small step for FIT 21, one big step for Crypto in America
the SEC's newfound focus on trading platforms raises the stakes while, ironically, making actual progress just as unlikely

items.rationale: Short/chunky lockups and fixed token allocations are read as VCs intending to 'dump their bags' on retail; the house pushes 4yr/1yr-cliff standards and dilutable allocations tied to decentralization milestones.

citations.quote: If a VC pushes to shorten your token lockup... what they're really telling you is 'we intend to dump our bags as soon as you'll let us'
If you're a founder in web3, you should never agree to fixed token allocations for investors... Fixed token allocations are predatory

items.rationale: Notable internal tension: even while bullish on ZK, the house warns SNARKs in production are 'riddled with bugs' and protected mainly by obscurity — called the biggest threat to SNARKs reaching their potential. Smart-contract dev remains hard and insecure.

citations.quote: the security issues cannot be overstated... they are the biggest threat to snarks not fulfilling their potential... they're riddled with bugs
Security bugs in ZK circuits will be challenging. Since only the proof is shared, the malicious input can't be scrutinized

items.rationale: MEV extraction is a persistent drain on users and a 'tax on using Ethereum'; faster block times reduce arbitrage MEV. Shared-sequencer monopolies add new ordering-rent risks.
items.age_note: Stated extensively 2023; not prominently restated in 2024

citations.quote: Mev remains what I would consider a problem for users users are losing a lot of money to arbitragers

items.rationale: Research shows flight-to-safety across stablecoins amplified by liquidity providers pulling out exactly when liquidity is most needed; algorithmic designs prone to death spirals; concentrated/centralized stablecoin regulation undermines decentralization.
items.age_note: Core research from Sept 2023; concentration concern restated Feb 2024

citations.quote: the flight to safety of the market triggers increased run risks on this kind of other stable coins... Amplified by the removal of liquidity
Concentrated power in the hands of any single stablecoin issuer undermines decentralization across web3

items.rationale: Concern that protocols hide a multisig or a dominant operator (Lido ~35% of stake) behind a decentralized facade; the house pushes negative-control multisigs and warns the economically rational outcome is value capture by teams unless true decentralization is enforced.
items.age_note: 2023 theme (Lido, multisig control models); echoed in 2024 ('hiding a multi-sig')

citations.quote: You are hiding a multi-sig in that decentralized network, are you not?
we end up with a lot of decentralization theater

items.rationale: Ignoring/profiting from money-laundering use (e.g. Samourai approach) is seen as inviting bans that would gut crypto's utility; the house favors compliance-provable privacy over absolutism.

citations.quote: if crypto privacy becomes synonymous with infinite money laundering/terrorist financing/sanctions avoidance we'll never reach critical mass

items.rationale: Treated as settled-decentralized and therefore not a security after the ETF approval; the bedrock of security for the rollup/restaking roadmap the house is building around.

citations.quote: These approvals enshrine the SEC's position in 2018 - ETH is not a security... decentralization moves tokens outside of the US securities law regime
The SEC capitulated on the question of whether ETH is a security in October when it approved the ETH futures products

items.rationale: Respected as a 'better gold' and the most successful crypto network; excitement centered on making Bitcoin programmable again via a SNARK-verify opcode and bringing ZK 'home' to it. Security model flagged as needing change long-term.
items.age_note: Programmability/ZK-on-Bitcoin pitch from Sept 2023; not heavily restated in 2024

citations.quote: adding a VERIFY_SNARK opcode to the bitcoin protocol — would make bitcoin totally programmable and free to move all over the internet
We should bring zero knowledge back home to bitcoin

items.rationale: Singled out as the emerging hub for the AI-x-crypto intersection the house is bullish on.

citations.quote: NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto

items.rationale: Thousands minted daily with no protocol, art, or game underneath — purely memetic/speculative; seen as damaging adoption, regulation, and builder behavior. Not technically interesting.

citations.quote: With thousands of memecoins minted daily, it's extremely hard to believe that the predominant activity is community focused
memecoins aren't very technically interesting. It shouldn't be a surprise that they're not attractive to builders

items.rationale: Repeatedly flagged as opaque about reserve quality; markets flee toward more transparent USDC/DAI under one type of stress but toward USDT to dodge US regulatory risk under another — a sign of underlying fragility/regulatory arbitrage.
items.age_note: From Sept 2023 stablecoin research; not restated in 2024

citations.quote: there were a lot of concerns about the quality of the assets of tether and which also was particularly not transparent about the quality of these assets

risk_regime.rationale: As of mid-2024 the house is structurally bullish and tactically constructive: its own State of Crypto Index and key adoption metrics (active addresses, mobile wallets, DEX volume) hit all-time highs in Mar–Apr 2024, FIT21 passed the House, and the ETH ETF was approved — a clear thaw from 2023's defensive, enforcement-besieged posture. Risk-on is qualified, not euphoric: leadership is openly hostile to the memecoin 'casino,' wary of predatory token structures, and still treats US regulatory uncertainty as the central overhang. The excitement is concentrated in building durable infrastructure and real-PMF apps (ZK, DePIN, payments, AI-x-crypto, modular Ethereum), not in speculation.

notes: Recency weighting: the dominant 2024 signals are (1) a strong pivot toward 'productive use cases vs. casino,' (2) a constructive read on US policy after FIT21/ETH-ETF reversing 2023's siege mentality (recorded in age_notes), and (3) concentrated technical excitement around ZK/zkVMs, DePIN, restaking/modular scaling, payments, and AI-x-crypto provenance. Internal tension worth flagging: the house is simultaneously the loudest bull AND loudest skeptic on ZK/SNARKs — bullish on zkVM potential while warning current SNARKs are 'riddled with bugs.' Provenance is split across two voices: a legal/regulatory thread (Miles Jennings et al. — SEC, Howey, decentralization, token lockups) and a tech/markets thread (Chris Dixon, Eddy Lazzarin, Justin Thaler, Scott Kominers, Ali Yahya — scaling, ZK, DePIN, network economics); they are coherent and mutually reinforcing, so 'consensus' is well-supported. Solana appears only as a positive adoption data point (most active addresses, low-fee DEX volume, Apr 2024), not an endorsement, so it is excluded from tokens_excited. Persistent-but-unrestated views (MEV, stablecoin run-risk, quantum threat, Lido/sequencer centralization) are cited at lower conviction per the recency rule. Conviction scores are intensity, not probability.
